sections.paragraph:
(kindred to the root חוּג) to go round in a circle, hence-

(1) to dance, 1Sa 30:16.

(2) to keep a festival, from the idea of leaping, and dancing in sacred dances, Exo 5:1 Lev 23:41 especially of a public assembly, Psal. 42:5 . (Syriac ܚܰܓܐܳ, ܚܰܓܝܺ id. Arabic حَجَّ to go to Mecca, as a Hadj or pilgrim, to keep the public festival.)

(3) to reel, to be giddy, used of drunkards, Psa 107:27 applied to a Person terrified, whence חָגָּא fear. Hence are derived חָג, חָגָּא and the proper names חַגַּי, חַגִּי, חַגִּיָה, חַגִּית.
